segments: PayPal Pay Later (buy now, pay later)
PayPal and Venmo branded debit and credit cards

dependencies: Reliance on third-party payment processors and financial institutions to process transactions
Dependence on payment card networks (credit and debit cards) for transaction processing
Reliance on external business partners and contractors for data center facilities, cloud computing, IT, and customer support
Dependence on third-party custodians for cryptocurrency safeguarding (licensed trust companies)
Reliance on partner financial institutions for U.S. consumer credit products and merchant financing (independent chartered financial institutions)
Dependence on banks and payment gateways linking to payment card and bank clearing networks
risks: Concentration risk with payment processors - two payment processors accounted for 48% of transaction expense in 2024
Operational, legal, regulatory, information security, reputational, commercial, and resiliency risks from third-party providers
Risk of third-party service providers being unable to meet obligations or experiencing disruptions
Cryptocurrency custodian risks including theft, loss, insufficient insurance, failure to maintain controls, inability to purchase/liquidate holdings
Bankruptcy risk of cryptocurrency custodians with uncertain treatment of custodial holdings
Risk of partner financial institution termination or interruption affecting U.S. consumer credit and merchant financing products
Supply chain disruptions mentioned as potential macroeconomic risk factor

regulatory_risks: Complex and evolving global regulatory environment for payments industry including anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing, privacy, cybersecurity, and consumer protection
Money transmitter licensing requirements in U.S. states, District of Columbia, and territories
Cryptocurrency regulation uncertainty - potential SEC classification of cryptocurrencies as securities requiring broker-dealer registration
PayPal USD stablecoin (PYUSD) regulatory scrutiny and evolving stablecoin regulations
Payment authentication requirements for banks and payment processors in various jurisdictions including U.S. and EU
Financial services regulation in EU (PayPal Europe as credit institution regulated by CSSF Luxembourg) and UK (PayPal UK as electronic money institution regulated by FCA)
Potential ECB direct supervision if PayPal Europe deemed significant supervised entity
ECB oversight under PISA framework for electronic payment instruments
Consumer lending regulations - federal and state laws governing consumer credit and debt collection in U.S., Australia, UK, France, Germany, Spain, Italy, Japan
CFPB direct supervisory authority as large market participant of remittance transfers
AML/CTF compliance requirements globally including AUSTRAC in Australia
Interchange fee regulations in EU (Multilateral Interchange Fee Regulation) and potential challenges in various jurisdictions
Data protection and privacy laws including GDPR-type regulations, California privacy laws, and evolving global privacy requirements
Data localization and residency laws in various countries
Anti-corruption laws including U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act
Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) reporting requirements including EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
Artificial Intelligence regulations including U.S. state regulations and EU Artificial Intelligence Act
Antitrust and competition law scrutiny in U.S. and internationally
Payment card network rules and potential violations or rule changes
Cross-border trade regulations including foreign exchange, tariffs, trade barriers, sanctions, import/export controls
Sanctions compliance including OFAC and equivalent authorities in other countries
Income tax regulations including OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax (15% rate) enacted in certain jurisdictions effective January 1, 2024
Potential regulatory changes to error resolution and chargeback requirements
Risk of regulatory actions, fines, penalties, enforcement actions, license revocations, and mandatory business practice changes

sanction_risks: Compliance with economic and trade sanctions administered by U.S., EU, UK, and other jurisdictions
OFAC sanctions compliance and potential violations
Risk of transactions with persons/entities on designated lists or in sanctioned countries
Voluntary reporting of potential compliance issues to OFAC and other regulators
Sanctions evasion risk through use of payments platform for illegal activities
Impact of U.S. and international trade relationships, tariffs, and restrictive actions on cross-border transactions
Geopolitical conditions affecting cross-border trade including military conflicts and terrorist attacks
Foreign exchange rate fluctuations due to geopolitical events
National or regional political, economic, or social instability in international operations
